macro_summary: Tuesday's session saw another volatile day, with the S&P 500 dipping 0.2% and the Nasdaq sliding 1.1% as tech-led selling resumed. The Reuters poll cemented expectations that the Fed will hold rates through 2026, pushing rate-cut hopes further out. Oil slipped slightly but the Hormuz crisis remains in its 99th day, while traders await Wednesday's CPI print — forecast to hit a multi-year high of 4.2% — which could dictate the next move.
commodities_note: WTI eased 0.4% to $88.72 as tentative Iran de-escalation hopes competed with Houthi Red Sea threats. Gold was flat at $4,196. The SOXX semiconductor ETF shows a 5.6% price conflict between data sources — its 5-day decline of -1.7% and 1-month gain of +14.2% are the more reliable trend indicators for the semis space.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

writeup.headline: DBS cools to 50-day after 4.9% weekly drop — back on watch.
writeup.what_to_do: DBS dropped 3.1% Tuesday, closing just 5% above its 50-day SMA at SGD 58.94, with RSI resetting from 66 to 53. The daily R:R is 1.1:1 to resistance at 65.19 — not yet favourable. NIM compression from US10Y above 4.20% and MAS S$NEER strength remains the structural headwind. Wait for a test of the 50-day with volume to confirm support before considering entry.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks are off their highs as rate-cut hopes fade; all three sit on WATCH with no entry triggers. The cluster needs US10Y to break below 4.20% for NIM relief — still a long way from the current 4.53%. OCBC's relative NIM resilience is the key differentiator vs DBS and UOB.
singapore.key_development: Singapore banks pulled back 2-3% in Tuesday's session, extending weekly losses to 2-5%. UOB's asset management unit sale talks could provide a near-term catalyst.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster remains in correction mode, with only NVDA and BE holding WATCH status. Extreme extension blocks dominate, and SOXX data is contested between sources. The structural AI demand is intact, but entry requires deeper cooldowns across the board.
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX daily data shows a 5.6% price-source gap; the 5-day -1.7% and 1-month +14.2% moves provide a more reliable read. AVGO's AI forecast miss continues to reverberate.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverges with GOOG holding WATCH on a strong R:R setup, while MSFT falls to CAUTION as the AI selloff intensifies and AMZN's capex overhang weighs. PLTR's deep drop signals fatigue with high-multiple AI software names.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but credit-circularity risk remains
big_tech.key_development: GOOG's $920M/month SpaceX compute deal and MSFT's OpenAI IPO filing are the key catalysts; MSFT's Azure credit circularity remains the big concern.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment spans Coherent as well — direct beneficiary of AI datacenter optical interconnects alongside LITE.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Q4 guidance confirms optical revenue acceleration above $500M quarterly run-rate.

interconnected.name: Corning
interconnected.reason: Amazon's multiyear fiber deal (per TheStreet, June 9) makes Corning a direct play on hyperscaler AI infrastructure buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Corning pulls back 10% from recent highs on a broader tech selloff.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Arista's networking switches are integral to NVDA's Spectrum-X back-end AI clusters — direct NVDA AI spend beneficiary.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA's next earnings confirm >$20B in AI networking revenue.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz crisis: US-Iran strikes continue, Houthis threaten Red Sea shipping; oil exports partially disrupted, day 99.
US-China tech decoupling: Pentagon blacklists Alibaba, BYD; Taiwan considers stricter AI chip export curbs to China.
Fed rate-hike expectations firm after 172K jobs beat; Reuters poll shows consensus for no rate cuts in 2026.
Tariff wildcard: Supreme Court ruling forces refunds; Trump proposes new 60-nation forced-labor tariffs.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $88.72 remains elevated but off highs, keeping an energy premium that lifts defense names (PLTR) and weighs on high-growth tech (GOOG, NVDA down 1-2%). US10Y at 4.53% sustains NIM pressure on Singapore banks. Semis are in correction as AI capex sustainability is questioned.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Houthis threaten Israeli shipping in the Red Sea (per Reuters, June 8). OpenAI confidentially filed for IPO, a week after Anthropic.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, WTI stays $85-95. Semis remain in correction, banks grind sideways. Fed holds rates; CPI today elevates volatility but no policy change.

optimistic.description: Ceasefire deal reopens Strait, oil drops below $80. Semis rally as energy costs ease, banks' NIM pressure eases with lower rates. Fed may cut once in late 2026.

pessimistic.description: Iran strikes US assets, Hormuz fully closed. Oil spikes to $120+, stocks sell off sharply. Safe havens surge; semis and banks crash on energy shock.

wildcard.description: China invades Taiwan or major cyberattack disrupts global supply chains, causing simultaneous semis supply shock and energy spike.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries until CPI print clarifies the rate path; defensive posture with cash ready for oversold opportunities.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — extreme extension blocks dominate semis, while banks hold as rates stay elevated.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 10 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension. The dominant risks are the Hormuz energy shock and the AI capex sustainability debate, both unresolved heading into Wednesday's CPI.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
